title: The Cultural Politics of Artificial Intelligence in China
author: Cai

opening_move.description: China's AI development (exemplified by Alibaba's City Brain) cannot be adequately explained by the dominant paradigm of neoliberal informationalism or surveillance capitalism, because it operates through a paradoxical state-capital nexus shaped by Leninist-Maoist traditions alongside market logics.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Understanding postsocialist AI as a distinct formation challenges the universalization of Western liberal frameworks and expands the political horizon of AI studies beyond homo economicus.

theoretical_positioning.description: The paper proposes 'postsocialist AI' as a concept that goes beyond neoliberal informationalism, using China's distinct state-capital nexus and Maoist-Leninist institutional heritage as a case that demands alternative theoretical resources.

epistemic_mode.qualitative_methods: Policy document analysis and secondary source analysis of Chinese smart city programs (City Brain, Shenzhen Net Weaving); draws on press and corporate whitepapers.
data_sources: BATH tech giants (Baidu, Alibaba, Tencent, Huawei)
epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Interpretive cultural analysis drawing on Parisi's ludic formalism, Wynter's critique of Western humanism, and Simondon's technical mentality to theorize Chinese AI epistemologically.
epistemic_mode.notes: The empirical material (policy documents, corporate data, project descriptions) is used to illustrate and motivate the theoretical argument about postsocialist AI. It is supportive rather than constitutive: the argument about distinct 'epistemological dispositifs' rests primarily on theoretical elaboration.

case_type.case_name: Alibaba City Brain and Chinese smart urbanism

normative_stake.domain: AI politics, postcolonial theory, democratic theory

confidence_notes: The paper occupies an interesting epistemic position: it combines a specific empirical case (Chinese AI) with theoretical elaboration drawing on philosophy of technology. The empirical material is real and specific (City Brain, policy documents) but serves to motivate and illustrate a theoretical argument. I classify it normative_with_qualitative_empirical with illustrative evidence role. The normative stake concerns expanding AI studies beyond Western liberal frameworks.
